name: Mexico
blurb: Co-hosts with passionate fans and decades of World Cup experience.
fact: Mexico will be the first nation to host the World Cup 3 times!
fact2: Mexico City is one of the largest cities in the world with over 21 million people

name: South Africa
blurb: Bafana Bafana back on the world stage with pace and flair.
fact: South Africa is the only African nation to host the World Cup
fact2: South Africa has 11 official languages

name: Korea Republic
blurb: Taegeuk Warriors with intensity, fitness and a flair for the dramatic.
fact: Korea Republic reached the semi-finals on home soil in 2002
fact2: 4 is considered unlucky in Korea because it looks like the symbol for death

name: Czechia
blurb: Technically gifted with a proud footballing history in Central Europe.
fact: Antonín Panenka from Czechia invented the famous chipped penalty kick
fact2: Czechia produces more beer per person than any other country in the world

name: Canada
blurb: Co-hosts making their mark after decades away from the World Cup.
fact: Canada's manager Jesse Marsch is actually from the USA
fact2: Canada is the second largest country in the world by area

name: Bosnia & Herz.
blurb: The Dragons with attacking flair and a passionate support.
fact: Bosnia & Herzegovina qualified for their first ever World Cup in 2014
fact2: As well as Bosnian, people there also speak Croatian and Serbian

name: Qatar
blurb: The 2022 hosts now competing as a regular tournament side.
fact: Qatar is the only country beginning with the letter Q
fact2: Qatar hosted the 2022 World Cup, the first in the Middle East

name: Switzerland
blurb: Quietly one of the most reliable and organised teams in world football.
fact: Switzerland have never won the World Cup but have hosted it twice
fact2: In 1908 Toblerone chocolate was founded in Switzerland

name: Brazil
blurb: Samba flair and five stars — always the team everyone watches.
fact: Brazilian player Pelé won 3 World Cups, more than anyone in history
fact2: Brazil is home to the Amazon rainforest, the largest in the world

name: Morocco
blurb: The Lions of Atlas dazzled the world in 2022 and want even more.
fact: In 2022 Morocco became the first African team to reach a World Cup semi-final
fact2: Morocco is in Africa but you can see Spain from its northern coast

name: Haiti
blurb: Les Grenadiers on the world stage for the first time in decades.
fact: Haiti were the first Caribbean nation to qualify for the World Cup in 1974
fact2: Haiti is home to the Citadelle Laferrière, one of the largest fortresses in the Americas

name: Scotland
blurb: The Tartan Army back on the world stage with pride and passion.
fact: Scotland were the first ever international football team, playing England in 1872
fact2: Scotland has over 790 islands around its coast

name: United States
blurb: Host nation with a rising generation hungry to impress on home soil.
fact: The USA hosted the World Cup in 1994 and averaged over 68,000 fans per game
fact2: The USA has more donut shops per person than any other country

name: Paraguay
blurb: La Albirroja with grit, resilience and South American heart.
fact: Paraguay have won the Copa América twice
fact2: Paraguay is one of only two landlocked countries in South America

name: Australia
blurb: The Socceroos punch above their weight with pace and determination.
bestFinish: 4th Place (2023 — Women's; Men's best: Round of 16 2006, 2022)
fact: Australia's football team is called the Socceroos in men's and the Matildas in women's
fact2: Australia is the only country that is also a continent

name: Türkiye
blurb: The Crescents with passion, quality and a knack for the dramatic.
fact: Türkiye finished 3rd in the 2002 World Cup, their best ever result
fact2: Istanbul in Türkiye is the only city in the world that sits on two continents

name: Germany
blurb: A classic powerhouse rebuilt and hungry to reclaim past glories.
fact: Miroslav Klose is the World Cup all-time top goalscorer with 16 goals
fact2: Germany is home to over 1,500 different types of beer

name: Curaçao
blurb: The island nation making history on the world's biggest stage.
fact: Curaçao has its own football team despite being owned by the Netherlands
fact2: Curaçao is famous for its bright blue liqueur used in cocktails around the world

name: Côte d'Ivoire
blurb: The Elephants charge with pace, power and a proud football tradition.
fact: Côte d'Ivoire reached the World Cup in 2006, 2010 and 2014
fact2: Côte d'Ivoire is one of the world's biggest producers of cocoa

name: Ecuador
blurb: La Tricolor bring passion and ambition from South America.
fact: Ecuador qualified for three consecutive World Cups between 2002 and 2014
fact2: Ecuador sits right on the equator — that's actually what Ecuador means in Spanish!

name: Netherlands
blurb: Oranje are back in style with a thrilling blend of youth and experience.
fact: The Netherlands have reached three World Cup finals but never won it
fact2: The Netherlands wear orange even though their flag doesn't have orange in it

name: Japan
blurb: Blue Samurai with remarkable technical quality and tireless work rate.
fact: Japan reached the last 16 at the 2022 World Cup, beating Germany and Spain
fact2: Japan created Nintendo, Pokémon and some of the world's best-selling cars

name: Sweden
blurb: Organised and physically powerful with quality in key positions.
fact: In 1958 Sweden got to the World Cup final, losing to Brazil
fact2: Sweden is home to IKEA, Spotify and Minecraft

name: Tunisia
blurb: The Eagles of Carthage representing Africa with pride and skill.
fact: Tunisia were the first African team to win a World Cup match, beating Mexico in 1978
fact2: Tunisia is home to some of the best preserved Roman ruins in the world

name: Belgium
blurb: A new golden generation emerging after the famous class of 2018.
fact: Belgium were ranked number 1 in the world for a record 5 years
fact2: Belgium actually invented French fries, not France!

name: Egypt
blurb: The Pharaohs powered by one of the world's most clinical forwards.
fact: Egypt have qualified for the World Cup 4 times
fact2: In 2018 Mohamed Salah won the Puskás Award for his goal against Chelsea

name: IR Iran
blurb: Team Melli with discipline, organisation and a knack for resilience.
fact: Iran have qualified for 7 World Cups
fact2: Iran is one of the oldest civilisations in the world, dating back over 7,000 years

name: New Zealand
blurb: The All Whites making the most of their Pacific football journey.
fact: New Zealand are known as the All Blacks in rugby but the All Whites in football
fact2: New Zealand was the first country in the world to give women the right to vote

name: Spain
blurb: La Roja reborn with a brilliant new crop of technically gifted players.
fact: Despite having amazing teams, Spain have only won the World Cup once, in 2010
fact2: Spain has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Europe

name: Cabo Verde
blurb: The Blue Sharks punching well above their weight on the world stage.
fact: Cabo Verde are making their World Cup debut in 2026
fact2: Cabo Verde is a group of volcanic islands off the west coast of Africa

name: Saudi Arabia
blurb: The Green Falcons who shocked Argentina in 2022 are back for more.
fact: In 2022 Saudi Arabia's captain Aldawsari scored the winner as they beat Argentina 2-1
fact2: Saudi Arabia is home to the world's largest oil company, Aramco

name: Uruguay
blurb: Garra Charrúa — fighting spirit and world-class quality combined.
fact: Uruguay hosted and won the very first World Cup in 1930
fact2: Uruguay was the first country in South America to legalise football on Sundays

name: France
blurb: Depth in every position with electric attacking talent throughout.
fact: France have won the World Cup twice, in 1998 at home and 2018 in Russia
fact2: France is the most visited country in the world

name: Senegal
blurb: African champions with power, pace and a world-class frontline.
fact: Senegal knocked out defending champions France in the 2002 World Cup group stage
fact2: Senegal is home to Lake Retba, a lake that is naturally pink in colour

name: Iraq
blurb: The Lions of Mesopotamia with a passionate fanbase behind them.
fact: Iraq qualified for the 2026 World Cup for only the second time in their history
fact2: Iraq is home to the ancient city of Babylon, one of the seven wonders of the ancient world

name: Norway
blurb: The rising force of European football built around a generational striker.
fact: Erling Haaland is from Norway and is one of the best strikers in the world
fact2: Norway is one of only a handful of countries where you can see the Northern Lights

name: Argentina
blurb: Defending champions with magic in their boots and fire in their hearts.
fact: In 1986 Diego Maradona scored the famous Hand of God goal for Argentina
fact2: Argentina is home to the widest avenue in the world, Avenida 9 de Julio in Buenos Aires

name: Algeria
blurb: The Desert Foxes with flair, pace and a fiercely loyal fanbase.
fact: Algeria qualified for five World Cups and reached the last 16 in 2014
fact2: Algeria is the largest country in Africa

name: Austria
blurb: Das Team — technically strong and full of Bundesliga quality.
fact: Austria got 4th in the 1934 World Cup and 3rd in 1954
fact2: Austria is the birthplace of Mozart, one of the greatest composers who ever lived

name: Jordan
blurb: The Nashama making their World Cup debut with pride and determination.
fact: Jordan qualified for the 2026 World Cup with a 3-0 win over Oman
fact2: The ancient city of Petra in Jordan was carved directly into rose-red rock over 2,000 years ago

name: Portugal
blurb: More than one superstar — a genuinely deep squad ready to compete.
fact: Portugal have never reached a World Cup final despite producing Eusébio and Cristiano Ronaldo
fact2: Portugal is the oldest country in Europe with the same borders since 1139

name: Congo DR
blurb: The Leopards — Africa's giant with raw talent and unstoppable energy.
fact: Congo DR used to be called Zaire and played in the 1974 World Cup
fact2: Congo DR is home to the second largest rainforest in the world

name: Uzbekistan
blurb: The White Wolves making a remarkable debut on the world stage.
fact: In 2025 Abdulkodir Khusanov became the first Uzbek player in the Premier League when he signed for Manchester City
fact2: Uzbekistan is home to the ancient Silk Road city of Samarkand

name: Colombia
blurb: Los Cafeteros with flair, creativity and passionate backing.
fact: Colombia have qualified for seven World Cups and reached the quarter-finals in 2014
fact2: Colombia has more species of birds than any other country in the world

name: England
blurb: Young Lions determined to finally bring football home.
fact: In the 1966 World Cup final Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick for England
fact2: England invented football and wrote the first ever rules of the game in 1863

name: Croatia
blurb: Chequered shirts and unbreakable spirit — always go further than expected.
fact: Croatia finished 2nd at the 2018 World Cup, their best ever result
fact2: Croatia has over 1,000 islands along its coastline

name: Ghana
blurb: Black Stars with a passionate fanbase and exciting attacking talent.
fact: Ghana came closest of any African team to a World Cup semi-final, losing on penalties to Uruguay in 2010
fact2: Ghana was the first country in sub-Saharan Africa to gain independence in 1957

name: Panama
blurb: Los Canaleros with heart, hustle and a never-say-die attitude.
fact: Panama are making their second ever World Cup appearance in 2026
fact2: The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans and saves ships 8,000 miles
